campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025, to review the city's finances from July, including how the city's investments performed and where the money went. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and returns
City spending and financial transactions from July
Campbell's multimodal transportation improvement plan
Future transportation options and infrastructure planning

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved to formally introduce the ordinance and skipped the usual first reading process to speed things up, planning to vote on final adoption at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a new ordinance
First reading of the ordinance was waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Community members had opportunity to speak during public hearing

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative tasks and moving forward with a construction project, though specific details about the project weren't fully visible in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager granted signing authority for the construction deal

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 14, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle several administrative and infrastructure matters. The council addressed staffing changes, appointed people to city boards and commissions, and approved amendments to contracts with engineering firms managing the city's wastewater facility upgrades and water recycling projects.
keyTopics: Mayor and council members requested excused absences from meetings
New appointments made to city boards and commissions
Contract updates with HDR Engineering and CDM Smith for wastewater facility construction oversight
Budget increase approved for the South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 project
Agreement amendment with Santa Clara Valley Habitat

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for infrastructure projects in the next fiscal year (2026-27), and then held a regular session where they recognized April as Teen Month and National Volunteer Week with official proclamations.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and planned city infrastructure projects
Official recognition of April as Teen Month in Mountain View
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to approve previous meeting notes and introduce a new rule about tenant protections in Sunnyvale. The commission discussed changes to Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing rules, which affects how renters are protected in the city.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance amending tenant protection rules (Chapter 19.71)
Residential Tenant Protections Program updates
Public hearing on housing regulations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026, to help residents learn about and provide feedback on OpenGov, a tool the city uses to show the public how it spends money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who try to influence city decisions on behalf of others to register and report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration and community input
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
How residents can track and engage with city budget decisions
Rules for who must disclose lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026 using a hybrid format, meaning residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The meeting information and instructions for submitting written comments to the council were made available to the public.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Public participation options for residents
Written comment submission process through eComment system
Meeting access details and contact information

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning documents and procedural items. The main focus was updating the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, reviewing new project requests from council members, and possibly changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development funding plan
New project ideas submitted by individual council members
Proposed changes to how council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting permission to add 5,000 square feet of additional floor space as part of seismic safety and historic preservation upgrades to the building.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building getting higher historic protection status
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area being added for renovations
Seismic safety improvements to the historic building
Downtown Commercial zone property undergoing rehabilitation
